project.title: Extracellular vesicles secreted from senescent cancer cells promote colorectal cancer progression through SERPINE1- LC­MS/MS analysis
project.projectDescription: By the LC-­MS/MS analysis, we identified the proteins that bind to SERPINE1.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The LC-MS/MS analysis was performed by BGI.  1.Proteolysis. （1）Soaked the gel in 50μL of pure water for 30 minutes and transferred to the corresponding EP tube;  (2) Added 1mL of decolorizing solution (50mM NH4HCO3: acetonitrile = 1:1, V:V), vortexed for 10 seconds, decolorized at 37°C for 30 minutes, briefly centrifuged and dried; repeated several times until the blue faded (Silver dyed gel does not need to be decolored, it can be washed directly with 1mL of pure water for 3 times); (3) Added 500μL of acetonitrile to dehydrate the gel to completely whiten, repeat once; soaked up the acetonitrile, opened the centrifuge tube and placed it in a clean bench for 10 minutes to air-dry acetonitrile, add 10mM DTT (1M DTT: 25mM NH4HCO3=1:100) until the liquid covered the gel, then put in water bath at 56°C for 1 hour; after cooling to room temperature, soaked it up, added 55mM IAM (0.55 M IAM:25mM NH4HCO3 = 1:10) quickly until the liquid covered the gel, and put at room temperature in a dark room for 45 minutes; (4) Soaked up the liquid, washed twice with 500μL of decolorizing solution (50 mM NH4HCO3: acetonitrile = 1:1, V:V), washed once with pure water; soaked up the liquid, added 500μL of acetonitrile, vortexed for 5 minutes. Soaked up the acetonitrile, opened the centrifuge tube lid and placed it on the clean bench for 10 minutes to dry thoroughly; (5) Diluted the enzyme concentration to 0.01μg/μL with 25mM NH4HCO3, and completely covered the gel with the enzyme solution, and stood at 4°C or ice for 30 minutes. After the gel was swelled, added the corresponding buffer to the gel. Incubated overnight at 37°C. (6) Added 5 times volume of 50% ACN the next day, vortexed for 5 minutes, centrifuged at 5,000g for 1 minute, transferred the supernatant to the new centrifuge tubes of the same number; added 5 volumes of 100% ACN, vortexed for 5 minutes. After centrifugation at 5,000g for 1 minute, the supernatant was transferred to a new centrifuge tube of the same number. Finally, the resulting supernatant was centrifuged at 25,000g for 5 minutes, and the supernatant was taken for freeze-dried.  2.HPLC. The dried peptide samples were reconstituted with mobile phase A (2% ACN, 0.1% FA), centrifuged at 20,000g for 10 minutes, and the supernatant was taken for injection. Separation was performed by Thermo UltiMate 3000 UHPLC. The sample was first enriched in trap column and desalted, and then entered a tandem self-packed C18 column (75μm internal diameter, 3μm column size, 25cm column length) and separated at a flow rate of 300nL/min through the following effective gradient: 0~5 minutes , 5% mobile phase B (98% ACN, 0.1% FA); 5~90 minutes, mobile phase B linearly increased from 5% to 26%; 90~100 minutes, mobile phase B increased from 26% to 35%; 100~108 minutes, mobile phase B rose from 35% to 80%; 108~113 minutes, 80% mobile phase B; 113~113.5 minutes, mobile phase B decreased to 5%; 113.5~120 minutes, 5% mobile phase B. The nanoliter liquid phase separation end was directly connected to the mass spectrometer.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The peptides separated by liquid phase chromatography were ionized by a nanoESI source and then passed to a tandem mass spectrometer Orbitrap Fusion Lumos (Thermo Fisher Scientific, San Jose, CA) for DDA (Data Dependent Acquisition) mode detection. The main parameters were set: ion source voltage was set to 2kV, MS1 mass spectrometer scanning range was 350~1,500m/z; resolution was set to 60,000; MS2 starting m/z was fixed at 100; resolution was 15,000. The ion screening conditions for MS2 fragmentation: charge 2+ to 6+, aynd the top 30 parent ions with the peak intensity exceeding 20,000. The ion fragmentation mode was HCD, and the fragment ions were detected in Orbitrap. The dynamic exclusion time was set to 30 seconds. The AGC was set to: MS1 1E5, MS2 2E4. The protein identification uses experimental MS/MS data and aligns them with theoretical MS/MS data from database to obtain results. The whole process starts from converting raw MS data into a peak list and then searching matches in the database. The search results are subject to strict filtering and quality control, and possible protein identifications are produced.
